data.Vulnerability: A stack-based buffer overflow in the genptk_text component in genptk.c of fig2dev 3.2.7b allows attackers to cause a denial of service (DOS) via converting a xfig file into ptk format.

data.Vulnerable function:
void genpstrx_text(F_text *text) { int x, y; char formatted_text[64*1024]; char angle[32]; /* print any comments prefixed with "%" */ print_comments("% ", text->comments, ""); if (Verbose) fprintf(tfp, "%% text - %s
", special_text(text) ? "special" : "no flags"); begin_set_font(text); convert_xy_to_image_bb(&x, &y, text->base_x, text->base_y); if (text->angle == 0) angle[0] = '\0'; else sprintf(angle, "{%.2lf}", text->angle * (180.0 / M_PI)); if (special_text(text)) { check_tex_format(text->cstring); fprintf(tfp, "\rput[%s]%s("DBL","DBL"){%s}%%
", ref_pt_str_from_text_type(text->type), angle, cm_from_1200ths(x), cm_from_1200ths(y), text->cstring); } else { fprintf(tfp, "\rput[%s]%s("DBL","DBL"){%s}%%
", ref_pt_str_from_text_type(text->type), angle, cm_from_1200ths(x), cm_from_1200ths(y), fmt_text_str(formatted_text, sizeof formatted_text, text->cstring, text->type)); } end_set_font(text); }
data.Vulnerability: A stack-based buffer overflow in the genpstrx_text() component in genpstricks.c of fig2dev 3.2.7b allows attackers to cause a denial of service (DOS) via converting a xfig file into pstricks format.

data.CWE Name: Out-of-bounds Write, Improper Restriction of Operations within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer
data.Vulnerability: An Out of Bounds flaw was found fig2dev version 3.2.8a. A flawed bounds check in read_objects() could allow an attacker to provide a crafted malicious input causing the application to either crash or in some cases cause memory corruption. The highest threat from this vulnerability is to integrity as well as system availability.

data.Vulnerable function: static int get_line(FILE *fp) { int len; while (1) { if (NULL == fgets(buf, BUFSIZ, fp)) { return -1; } ++line_no; if (*buf == '#') { if (save_comment() < 0) return -1; } else if (*buf != '\n') { len = strlen(buf); buf[len-1] = '\0'; if (buf[len-2] == '\r') buf[len-2] = '\0'; return 1; } } }
data.Vulnerability: A buffer underwrite vulnerability in get_line() (read.c) in fig2dev 3.2.7a allows an attacker to write prior to the beginning of the buffer via a crafted .fig file.

data.Vulnerable function: static void put_patternarc(F_pos *c, double rad, double da, double angle1, F_arc *a) { int i, cosa2, sina2; double a1, a2, rot, cosr, sinr; F_spline s; F_line *l; F_point *p; F_point o[8]; F_control z[8]; struct d_pos u[8]; get_xsplinepoints(z, u, 1.025821); for (i = 0; i < 8; ++i) { o[i].x = round(rad * u[i].x); o[i].y = round(rad * u[i].y); o[i].next = &(o[i+1]); } o[i-1].next = NULL; s.type = T_CLOSED_XSPLINE; s.style = a->style; s.thickness = a->thickness; s.pen_color = a->pen_color; s.depth = a->depth; s.fill_style = UNFILLED; s.style_val = a->style_val; s.for_arrow = a->for_arrow; s.back_arrow = a->back_arrow; s.cap_style = a->cap_style; s.points = &(o[0]); s.controls = &(z[0]); s.comments = NULL; s.next = NULL; l = create_line_with_spline(&s); l->join_style = MITERJOIN; p = l->points; if (p == NULL) return; a1 = atan2(p->y, p->x); a2 = a1 - da; sinr = sin(a2); cosr = cos(a2); sina2 = round(rad * sinr); cosa2 = round(rad * cosr); #define WIND_FORWARD o[3].next = p; p = p->next for (;;) { if (a2 < M_PI_4) { while (p->y > p->x) {WIND_FORWARD;} } else { while (p->x < cosa2) {WIND_FORWARD;} break; } if (a2 < -M_PI_4) { while (p->x > -p->y) {WIND_FORWARD;} } else { while (p->y > sina2) {WIND_FORWARD;} break; } if (a2 < -3. * M_PI_4) { while (p->y < p->x) {WIND_FORWARD;} } else { while (p->x > cosa2) {WIND_FORWARD;} break; } if (a2 < -5. * M_PI_4) { while (p->x < -p->y) {WIND_FORWARD;} while (p->x < cosa2) {WIND_FORWARD;} break; } else { while (p->y < sina2) {WIND_FORWARD;} break; } } #undef WIND_FORWARD rot = sqrt((double)o[3].next->x * o[3].next->x + (double)o[3].next->y * o[3].next->y); o[1].x = round(rot * cosr); o[1].y = round(rot * sinr); o[1].next = NULL; o[4].next = p; o[3].next->next = &(o[1]); if (a->direction) { i = -1; rot = YDIR(angle1 - M_PI + a1); } else { i = 1; rot = YDIR(angle1 - a1); } cosr = cos(rot); sinr = sin(rot); p = l->points; while (p != NULL) { int px; px = i * p->x; p->x = c->x + round(cosr * px - YDIR(sinr * p->y)); p->y = c->y + round(sinr * px + YDIR(cosr * p->y)); p = p->next; } if (a->type == T_PIE_WEDGE_ARC) { l->type = T_POLYGON; o[0].x = c->x; o[0].y = c->y; o[0].next = l->points; o[1].next = &(o[2]); o[2].x = c->x; o[2].y = c->y; o[2].next = NULL; l->points = &(o[0]); } else { l->type = T_POLYLINE; } i = verbose; genpict2e_line(l); verbose = i; o[3].next->next = o[4].next; if (a->type == T_PIE_WEDGE_ARC) l->points = o[0].next; free_linestorage(l); }
data.Vulnerability: Segmentation fault in fig2dev in version 3.2.9a allows an attacker to availability via local input manipulation via put_patternarc function.
In xfig diagramming tool, a stack-overflow while running fig2dev allows memory corruption via local input manipulation via read_objects function.
